title: agrostis capillaris subsp. castellana
intro: Agrostis capillaris subsp. castellana, also known as Castilian bent or Castilian bent-grass, is a subspecies of grass native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Agrostis capillaris subsp. castellana can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
